title: carya texana
intro: Carya texana, also known as the Black Hickory, is a deciduous tree native to the southeastern United States. It is prized for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds that have been used to treat a variety of ailments, such as skin irritations, wounds, and fever. The health benefits of Carya texana are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, promote healing, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of Carya texana can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, and are often used to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne.
